commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blockers are maintainer product/security/automation ownership and contributor proof, not a safe automated repair lane.; Needs attention: No remaining line-level credential leak was confirmed, but the PR introduces a new first-party mobile credential/WebView/direct-provider security boundary that needs maintainer acceptance. (sha=434ae516df9bf15cff06a0b0d36422e97326b095)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a native SwiftUI iOS client with CrabboxKit, an embedded Go CLI bridge, islo sandbox/map-reduce/LLM flows, mobile docs/scripts/assets, and mobile CI jobs.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new feature PR rather than a bug report. The merge-readiness check is proof and product/security acceptance, not reproducing a current-main failure.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are maintainer product/security/automation ownership and contributor proof, not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: feat: native SwiftUI Crabbox iOS app (crabbox.sh client + on-device/sandbox LLM + islo)
target.body: ## 🦀 Crabbox for iOS — *a short-lived box for every `run`*, now from your phone Evolves `mobile/` into a genuinely **native SwiftUI** app — a Crabbox **command center** that warms boxes, runs work across them, and reduces the results, with the **real Crabbox Go CLI compiled into the app** and Apple's on-device LLM on board. No web wrapper, no Expo. > Provider scope: **islo.dev only.** crabbox.sh is the broker; islo.dev is where the boxes run. --- ### 🧮 Map-Reduce from the phone (the headline) One tap fans a computation across N islo sandboxes (**MAP**) and aggregates on the phone (**REDUCE**). Live demo — `Σ(1…1000)` + min/max across **3 islo sandboxes**: ``` $ map-reduce Σ(1…1000) over 3 islo sandbox(es) [probe] crabbox-mapreduce-demo-{0,1,2} ✓ live [map] demo-2 → sum=55611 min=1 max=333 (1…333) [map] demo-0 → sum=166500 min=334 max=666 (334…666) [map] demo-1 → sum=278389 min=667 max=1000 (667…1000) [reduce] Σ = 55611 + 166500 + 278389 = 500500 · min 1 · max 1000 ✅ ``` Verified live against `api.islo.dev`. The runner probes the targets, **re-shards across only the live boxes** (so the total is always correct), runs each shard via `IsloClient.exec` in parallel, and reduces on-device. A 4-box run reduces `31375 + 93875 + 156375 + 218875 = 500500` the same way. --- ### ✨ Everything it does | | Use case | Where | |---|---|---| | 🧮 | **Map-reduce / command center** — shard work across N islo boxes, reduce on the phone | Run | | 🖥️ | **Run a Crabbox command on islo** — embedded Go core runs `crabbox run --provider islo …` **in-process** | Run | | 📦 | **Sandbox lifecycle** — list / pause / resume / delete islo boxes | Sandboxes | | 🔗 | **crabbox.sh as broker** — coordinator provisions & runs on islo-backed workspaces | Run / Sandboxes | | 🧠 | **LLM** — Apple **Foundation Models** on-device (verified on an iPhone 16) + Ollama on a leased islo box | Assistant | | 🌐 | **Portal** — native WebView of **crabbox.sh** | Portal | | 🔐 | **Secrets in the Keychain** | Settings | ### 📱 Screens | Run — command center | Sandboxes — live islo leases | |:--:|:--:| | | | | **Assistant — on-device LLM** | **Portal — live crabbox.sh** | | | | --- ### 🏗️ How the Go core runs on iOS iOS can't spawn a separate `crabbox` binary, so the Go engine is compiled into the app as a **`-buildmode=c-archive` static library** (`CrabboxMobile`) and called via a tiny C/Swift bridge: ``` Swift ──JSON──▶ CrabboxMobileRun(*C.char) ──▶ cli.App{}.Run(ctx, args) // the real internal/cli ◀─JSON── {exitCode, stdout, stderr} + mobile-safe islo provider (HTTPS to islo.dev) ``` **CrabboxKit** is the pure, testable Swift core (`IsloClient`, `CoordinatorClient`, provisioners, LLM engines) — builds on macOS **and** Linux. **App** is the SwiftUI surface (Run / Sandboxes / Assistant / Portal) in a polished dark Crabbox theme. ### ✅ Validated end-to-end - **Builds, installs, and runs on a physical iPhone 16 (iOS 26.5)** and the **iOS 17 simulator** — Go core links on both (device link needs `-lresolv`; the iOS SDK's `libresolv.9.tbd` exports the `res_9_*` symbols Go uses). - **Map-reduce over real islo sandboxes → 500500** (above). - Apple on-device LLM (Foundation Models) confirmed working on the iPhone 16. - `swift build` · `go build ./mobile/go/crabboxmobile` · `swift run crabbox-sim` (18 scenarios, 0 invariant violations). ### 🔧 Also in this revision - **islo auth hardening**: `IsloClient` retries once on HTTP 401 (re-exchanges the API key) so a session JWT expiring/rotating mid-run doesn't fail the map-reduce. - **LLM readiness**: a launched sandbox engine isn't offered for chat until Ollama is actually serving (poll `isReady()` before registering). - Writable `XDG_STATE_HOME`/`HOME` for the Go core's lease-claim system in the iOS sandbox. - Sandbox targeting via `--slug` (correct `run` flag). - Fixed a Portal crash on malformed `mailto:`/`tel:` URLs. - **UI polish**: refined palette + brighter mint, 8-pt spacing, haptics, terminal styling, springy buttons; **chat keyboard dismissal** (swipe, tap, Done toolbar, dismiss-on-send). ### 📦 Install on a device ```sh cd mobile DEVELOPMENT_TEAM=XXXXXXXXXX ./scripts/install-on-device.sh ``` 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: zozo123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: vincentkoc
